title: American Music Tourism Act of 2025
plain_summary: The American Music Tourism Act of 2025 gives new jobs to a government office that helps promote tourism in the United States. This office must now work to bring more visitors from America and other countries to music festivals, concert halls, and places important to music history. The law also expands the office's work with sports tourism to include bringing international visitors to sports events in the US. The office has to write a report to Congress about this work within one year, and then write a new report every two years after that.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the American Music Tourism Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Responsibilities of the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Travel and Tourism
sections.text: This section changes the Visit America Act to give new duties to the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Travel and Tourism. For domestic travel and tourism, the Assistant Secretary must now find places and events in the United States that are important for music tourism. Music tourism means traveling to see music-related places or attend music events. The Assistant Secretary must help promote travel to these music places and events within the United States. For international business and leisure travel, the Assistant Secretary must work with other federal agencies to bring more visitors from other countries to the United States. This includes helping with large meetings, conferences, and exhibitions. The Assistant Secretary must focus on rural areas and other places in America that have rich cultural heritage or nature tourism. The Assistant Secretary must also promote international travel to sports and recreation events in the United States. Most importantly, the Assistant Secretary must find places and events that are important for music tourism and help promote international travel to these music destinations. The Assistant Secretary must submit a report to Congress no later than one year after this law passes and every two years after that. The report must go to the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation and the House Committee on Energy and Commerce. The report must describe activities, findings, achievements, and problems related to these tourism goals. This section also defines music tourism as traveling to a state or local area to visit music-related attractions like museums, studios, venues of all sizes, and other music sites. It also means traveling to attend music festivals, concerts, or other live musical performances or special music events.
